question.assertion: The first ionization energy of nitrogen is higher than that of oxygen.
question.reason: Nitrogen has a stable half-filled $2p^3$ configuration which provides extra stability due to exchange energy.

options.A: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
options.D: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)

Periodic Trends Advanced.question: Which of the following represents the correct trend for the mentioned property?

statements.A: F > Cl > Br > I — First Ionization Energy
statements.B: S > O > Se > Te — Electron Affinity (magnitude)
statements.C: Li < Na < K < Rb — Atomic radius
statements.D: B < C < N < O — First Ionization Energy

Periodic Trends Advanced.question: Among the following species: $Na^+, Mg^{2+}, Al^{3+}, O^{2-}, F^-, Ne, N^{3-}$. The number of species that are isoelectronic with Ne and have ionic radius greater than Ne atomic radius is ____.

question.statement_1: The correct order of first ionization enthalpy is $B < Be < N < O$.
question.statement_2: The correct order of atomic radii is $Li > B > C > N$.

options.A: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
options.B: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
options.C: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
options.D: Statement I is true but Statement II is false

question.statement_1: The correct order of electronegativity (Pauling scale) is $F > O > Cl > N$.

options.B: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
options.C: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

question.assertion: The first ionization enthalpy of nitrogen is higher than that of oxygen.
question.reason: Nitrogen has a stable half-filled $2p^3$ configuration, and removing an electron from this configuration requires extra energy.

options.C: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
options.D: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)

Periodic Trends Advanced.question: Which of the following statements are correct? I. Electron gain enthalpy of fluorine is less negative than that of chlorine. II. The second ionization enthalpy of sodium is much higher than its first ionization enthalpy. III. Across a period, both ionization enthalpy and electron gain enthalpy generally increase. IV. Among halogens, iodine has the highest electron gain enthalpy.

question.assertion: The first ionization enthalpy of Be is higher than that of B.
question.reason: Be has a completely filled $2s$ orbital which is more stable than the partially filled $2p$ orbital of B.

options.B: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation
options.C: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation

Coordination Compounds Focus.question: Given below are two statements: Statement I: Among $[Fe(H_2O)_6]^{2+}, [Fe(CN)_6]^{4-}, [Co(NH_3)_6]^{3+}$, and $[CoF_6]^{3-}$, the complex $[CoF_6]^{3-}$ has the maximum number of unpaired electrons. Statement II: Both $[Fe(CN)_6]^{4-}$ and $[Co(NH_3)_6]^{3+}$ are diamagnetic in nature. Choose the correct answer:

options.A: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
options.B: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.C: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
options.D: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

Coordination Compounds Focus.question: Given below are two statements: Statement I: Among $[Pt(CN)_4]^{2-}, [NiCl_4]^{2-}, [Pd(NH_3)_4]^{2+}$, and $[CuCl_4]^{2-}$, only two complexes exhibit square planar geometry. options.B: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
options.C: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
options.D: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
